news.paragraph: AMD is navigating positive momentum following a blowout Q1 earnings report that has sparked discussions regarding its future growth potential. CEO Lisa Su has signaled confidence in the company's AI strategy, suggesting that CPUs will play a larger role in the next phase of AI development. Traders should weigh this recent earnings success and the company's positioning in the expanding AI server market when considering new positions.
headlines: Does AMD Still Have Room to Climb After Blowout Q1 Earnings?
AMD CEO Lisa Su Says CPUs Will Match GPUs 1-to-1 in AI’s Next Phase
Was Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) One of the Top “Blue Chip” Stocks Suddenly on Fire in April?
Intel and AMD Poised to Dominate $135 Billion AI Server Market
What AMD's Earnings Beat Could Mean for Intel
AMD CEO Lisa Su Got Asked 'Do You Speak English' At A Formula One Race Where The Chipmaker Was Sponsoring Ferrari: Here's How She Handled That
